chapter: Chapter 3: HOW THE STATE GOVERNMENT WORKS

questions.question: What is the full form of MLA?
questions.answer: The full form of MLA is Member of the Legislative Assembly.

questions.question: How is an MLA elected?
questions.answer: MLAs are elected by the people. They represent a specific area called a constituency and win through an election process.

questions.question: What is a 'Constituency'?
questions.answer: A constituency is a particular area from which all the voters living there choose their representative.

questions.question: Which political party is called the 'Ruling Party'?
questions.answer: The political party that has more than half the number of MLAs in a state is considered to be in a majority and is called the ruling party.

questions.question: Who are the 'Opposition' in the Legislative Assembly?
questions.answer: All the elected representatives who are not members of the ruling party are called the opposition.

questions.question: Who appoints the Chief Minister and other ministers?
questions.answer: The Governor of the state appoints the Chief Minister and other ministers.

questions.question: What is the role of the Governor in a state?
questions.answer: The Governor is the Head of the State. They are appointed by the Central Government to ensure that the State Government works within the rules of the Constitution.

questions.question: What is a 'Legislative Assembly'?
questions.answer: A Legislative Assembly is a place where all the MLAs, whether from the ruling party or from the opposition, meet to discuss various things.

questions.question: Who has the main responsibility of running various government departments or ministries?
questions.answer: The Chief Minister and other ministers have the responsibility of running various government departments or ministries.

questions.question: What happens during a debate in the Legislative Assembly?
questions.answer: During a debate, MLAs can express their opinions, ask questions about issues, and give suggestions about what the government should do.

questions.question: What is a 'Press Conference'?
questions.answer: A press conference is a gathering of journalists who are invited to hear about and ask questions on a particular issue and are expected to report on this to the larger public.

questions.question: How does the government respond to issues raised in the assembly?
questions.answer: The concerned minister responds to the questions and tries to assure the Assembly that adequate steps are being taken.

questions.question: Name two ways people express their views about the government.
questions.answer: People express their views through newspapers, TV channels, protest rallies, and press conferences.

questions.question: Who is the head of the executive at the state level?
questions.answer: The Chief Minister is the head of the executive at the state level.

questions.question: Can the government make new laws for the state?
questions.answer: Yes, the government makes laws on certain issues like health and sanitation in the Legislative Assembly of each state.

questions.question: What is a 'Coalition' government?
questions.answer: When no single party gets a majority and two or more parties come together to form a government, it is called a coalition government.

questions.question: Who oversees the work done by the various government departments?
questions.answer: The work done by various departments has to be approved by the members of the Legislative Assembly.

questions.question: Give an example of a government department.
questions.answer: Examples include the School Education Department, the Agriculture Department, and the Public Works Department.

questions.question: Why do MLAs meet in a Legislative Assembly?
questions.answer: They meet to discuss problems in their constituencies, debate on state issues, and pass laws for the welfare of the people.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a Wallpaper project in schools mentioned in the book?
questions.answer: The wallpaper project is an interesting activity where research can be done on particular topics of interest to help students understand how government works.

questions.question: Does an MLA only belong to the ruling party?
questions.answer: No, an MLA can belong to the ruling party, the opposition party, or can be an independent candidate.

questions.question: Who is responsible for answering questions asked by the media?
questions.answer: The Chief Minister and the ministers in charge of specific departments are responsible for answering questions from the media.

questions.question: What happens if a party gets exactly half the seats?
questions.answer: A party needs more than half the seats to have a majority. If it has exactly half or less, it must seek support from other parties or independent MLAs to form a government.

questions.question: In the story in the book, why were the people protesting?
questions.answer: In the story, people were protesting because of the lack of proper health facilities and the spread of diarrhea in their area.

questions.question: How do we know who is the Chief Minister of a state?
questions.answer: The Chief Minister is the leader of the ruling party who is chosen by the elected MLAs of that party and then appointed by the Governor.
